V-M-076.scope: Phase-58/Phase-59 premium admin cockpit evidence remains valid. Phase-62 verification now defines the compactness and deletion-audit contract for admin cockpit, MTProto analytics, operational inventories, emergency controls, redaction, responsive regressions, and protected backend/deploy/runtime isolation.

module-checks.check-9: Chrome screenshot audit for frontend-admin /login, /, /users, /devices, /mtproto, /servers, /plans, and /analytics at 360x740, 390x844, 430x932, 768x1024, 1440x900, plus reduced-motion / 390x844. Local pass used static route proof because no authenticated admin backend session was available; clean-deploy authenticated screenshot review remains follow-up.
module-checks.check-10: python3 XML parse for docs/modules/M-076.xml docs/plans/Phase-58.xml docs/verification/V-M-076.xml docs/verification-index.xml
module-checks.check-13: git diff --name-only HEAD -- install.sh docker-compose.yml nginx deploy deploy/mtproto-de-compose.yml .env.example backend/app backend/tests mtproto-runtime official-mtproxy frontend must be empty
module-checks.check-22: python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/plans/Phase-62.xml docs/modules/M-076.xml docs/verification/V-M-076.xml docs/verification-index.xml
module-checks.check-23: mygrace lint; git diff --check; protected diff guard for backend/deploy/runtime/install/frontend-user surfaces
module-checks.check-24: Phase-58 regression guard allows only frontend/src/components/VisualShell.tsx as shared Matrix reliability diff and remains green through node scripts/phase60-premium-qa-smoke.mjs.
module-checks.check-29: protected backend/deploy/runtime/frontend-user/tariff guard proved Phase-74 is admin-shell-only.
module-checks.check-31: node scripts/phase75-admin-users-devices-smoke.mjs passed for unified Users inventory, removed Devices nav, direct /devices safety, expandable device rows, action confirmations, and protected guard.
module-checks.check-32: node scripts/phase58-premium-admin-cockpit-smoke.mjs and node scripts/phase62-compactness-deletion-smoke.mjs remain final-run regressions after the merge.

classification.remove: Duplicated KPI cards, repeated explanatory copy, decorative-only wrappers, stale helper text, and status summaries that do not expose unique admin state or action.
classification.collapse: Secondary analytics details, long history blocks, verbose operation notes, alert archive details, and low-frequency metadata behind bounded panels, tabs, drawers, or expandable rows.
classification.move-secondary: Verbose admin descriptions, secondary filters, low-priority archive views, and deep troubleshooting text when they compete with first-screen service health or incident/action surfaces.
classification.compact: Dashboard, users, devices, MTProto, MTProto analytics, servers, plans, and analytics pages using denser rows, bounded inventories, compact chart frames, and mobile-first action grouping.
classification.keep: Emergency controls, destructive/disruptive confirmations, user/device actions, MTProto disable/reissue/revoke/block/IP alert workflows, alert archive access, promotion tag operations, redacted analytics, server health, canonical tariffs, logout, and admin redaction.

preservation-invariants.invariant-1: Admin health, users, devices, MTProto, alerts, servers, plans, and analytics remain reachable within one or two taps on phone layouts.
preservation-invariants.invariant-2: Emergency and destructive controls must remain visible with explicit confirmation text after any compaction or deletion.
preservation-invariants.invariant-3: MTProto analytics keeps useful admin signals while removing technical noise from visible UI; no secrets, full proxy links, bearer tokens, provider keys, or raw payment identifiers may appear in smoke output.
preservation-invariants.invariant-4: Phase-62 must not change backend admin APIs, auth/session behavior, billing catalog ownership, VPN/MTProto runtime, deploy/install scripts, nginx, docker-compose, secrets, or production topology.

verification-matrix.target-1: Admin layout keeps compact navigation, authenticated admin context, logout, route active state, and emergency surfaces reachable on phone/tablet/desktop.
verification-matrix.target-2: Dashboard first viewport exposes service health, users, devices, revenue/billing, VPN, MTProto, analytics, and incident signals without decorative bulk or horizontal scroll.
verification-matrix.target-3: Users and devices pages preserve search, filters, bounded scrolling, readable dense rows, details, block/unblock or device-control actions, and explicit confirmation for destructive/disruptive operations.
verification-matrix.target-4: MTProto and MTProto analytics preserve redacted assignment/secret/promotion-tag displays, confirmation-safe reissue/revoke/disable/block actions, alert inbox/archive, IP-history compactness, charts, top users, runtime health, and auto-refresh markers.
verification-matrix.target-5: Plans page preserves canonical M-068 tariff matrix and does not introduce accidental CRUD controls, frontend-owned checkout amounts, or catalog mutation paths.
verification-matrix.target-6: Servers and analytics pages stay operationally dense while preserving runtime-data read-only semantics, health visibility, and no backend API shape changes.
verification-matrix.target-7: Phone/tablet/desktop/reduced-motion screenshots show no text/icon/button/status overlap, no hidden emergency CTA, no long unbounded inventory, and no Matrix layer click/scroll interception.
verification-matrix.target-8: Phase-75 Users route becomes the single account/device inventory, with expandable device rows and preserved emergency device actions after the separate Devices nav is retired.

phase-74-admin-cockpit-gates.gate-1: Admin Layout navigation order must place Logout directly under Nodes and remove visible Tariffs/Plans from the rail.
phase-74-admin-cockpit-gates.gate-2: Dashboard, Users, MTProto, Servers/Nodes, and Analytics admin cockpit routes must remain reachable, compact, and operational.
phase-74-admin-cockpit-gates.gate-3: Direct Plans access must be safely redirected, hidden, or otherwise handled without a broken blank screen.
phase-74-admin-cockpit-gates.gate-4: Admin scroll repair must allow normal content-area wheel scrolling and bound admin-rail overflow.
phase-74-admin-cockpit-gates.gate-5: Canonical tariffs remain protected by M-068 and are no longer an admin cockpit visible route after Phase-74.

phase-75-admin-cockpit-gates.gate-1: Admin cockpit must expose one "Пользователи" inventory that combines account rows and expandable device sublists.
phase-75-admin-cockpit-gates.gate-2: Visible "Устройства" navigation is removed only after block/unblock/rotate/revoke device controls are reachable from expanded user rows.
phase-75-admin-cockpit-gates.gate-3: Direct /devices must redirect or remain a compatibility path without creating a duplicate long-scroll device dashboard.
phase-75-admin-cockpit-gates.gate-4: Users inventory must support search, pagination, loading/empty/error states, and bounded expanded device lists for large accounts.
phase-75-admin-cockpit-gates.gate-5: Emergency device actions, confirmation text, feedback, and audit-safe behavior must be preserved.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: Admin can reach dashboard health, users, devices, MTProto, plans, servers, analytics, and incident review in one or two taps on phone.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: Users/devices/MTProto inventories are searchable, bounded, compact, and remain readable without losing row actions.
success-scenarios.scenario-3: Destructive and disruptive admin actions retain visible confirmation text, guarded buttons, and audit-safe feedback.
success-scenarios.scenario-4: MTProto secrets, full tg links, raw promotion tags when masked, access tokens, raw IP history exports, and payment/provider identifiers are not printed by smoke output, console markers, screenshots, or docs.
success-scenarios.scenario-5: Phase-58 does not touch backend admin APIs, auth/session handling, deploy/install scripts, nginx, docker-compose, MTProto/VPN runtime, or canonical tariff catalog behavior.

failure-scenarios.failure-1: Premium visual redesign hides emergency controls, destructive confirmations, MTProto reissue/revoke/disable/block actions, user/device actions, tariff state, server health, or incident review.
failure-scenarios.failure-2: Any admin route exposes MTProto secrets, full proxy links, raw promotion tags, bearer/refresh tokens, raw emails beyond synthetic fixtures, payment IDs, YooKassa/Resend keys, or backend secrets.
failure-scenarios.failure-3: Users, devices, MTProto, analytics, or plans become unbounded long-scroll surfaces without search/filter/scroll containment.
failure-scenarios.failure-4: Plans page introduces canonical tariff mutation, frontend-controlled payment amounts, or accidental CRUD controls.
failure-scenarios.failure-5: Phase-58 changes backend API shape, auth/session behavior, billing catalog values, MTProto/VPN runtime behavior, deploy/install scripts, nginx, docker-compose, or production topology.
failure-scenarios.failure-6: Phase-75 removes the separate Devices page before device actions are reachable and confirmation-safe inside the Users route.
failure-scenarios.failure-7: Phase-75 leaves both Users and Devices as duplicate primary inventories, making admin navigation longer instead of simpler.

telemetry-requirements.requirement-1: Allowed smoke output: route names, viewport names, selector markers, action names, redacted state names, screenshot paths, and pass/fail markers.
telemetry-requirements.requirement-2: Forbidden smoke output: MTProto ee secrets, full tg:// or https://t.me proxy links, raw promotion tags when masked in UI, bearer/refresh tokens, reset/verify tokens, real user emails, raw IP export data, payment IDs, YooKassa/Resend keys, database URLs, and API keys.
telemetry-requirements.requirement-3: If screenshots require authenticated fixtures, fixtures must use synthetic admin/user/device/proxy/payment data only; live clean-deploy screenshots must be operator-approved and redacted.

phase-gates.gate-1: M-076 smoke proves admin dashboard, layout, users, devices, MTProto, MTProto analytics, plans, servers, analytics, compact primitives, confirmation guards, and redaction guards.
phase-gates.gate-2: M-010, M-037, M-047, M-058, M-068, M-070, M-071, and M-072 regression smokes remain satisfied after admin cockpit edits.
phase-gates.gate-3: Frontend-admin build, Phase-58 smoke, regression smokes, screenshot/static route proof, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff check, and protected backend/deploy/runtime/frontend-user guard pass before Phase-58 can be marked done-local.
phase-gates.gate-4: Phase-62 passed locally on 2026-06-04: inventory, classification, prune/collapse decisions, preserved workflow proof, static-route proof, frontend-admin build, Phase-62 smoke, Phase-58/54/61/60 regressions, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff check, and protected backend/deploy/runtime/frontend-user guard passed.
phase-gates.gate-5: Authenticated screenshots may use static-route proof locally; live clean-deploy authenticated screenshot review remains required before production-signoff claims.
phase-gates.gate-6: Phase-58 guard update is limited to shared Matrix VisualShell reliability diff and does not permit backend/deploy/runtime drift.
phase-gates.gate-7: Phase-74 admin build and shell smoke passed; Phase-54/58/59/61/62 regressions, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, and git diff --check remain required in the final verification run.
phase-gates.gate-8: Phase-75 passed unified Users inventory, removed Devices nav, safe direct /devices handling, preserved device actions, and bounded expanded rows through frontend-admin build and Phase-75 smoke.

evidence.evidence-1: 2026-06-04 pass-local: Phase-58 smoke printed all required PremiumAdminCockpit markers including routes, command center, actions, bounded inventories, MTProto redaction, confirmation guards, tariff guard, readonly analytics, reduced motion, static screenshot proof, and protected-surface guard.
evidence.evidence-2: Regression evidence passed: Phase-42 MTProto admin analytics smoke, Phase-43 MTProto analytics UX/IP/alerts smoke, Phase-50 tariff catalog smoke, Phase-52 Matrix visual smoke, Phase-54 admin Matrix smoke, and Phase-55 premium art-direction smoke.
evidence.evidence-3: Protected-surface evidence passed: no diff in install.sh, docker-compose.yml, nginx, deploy, deploy/mtproto-de-compose.yml, .env.example, backend/app, backend/tests, mtproto-runtime, official-mtproxy, or frontend.
evidence.evidence-4: 2026-06-04 pass-local: Phase-59 admin motion smoke passed for Devices, MTProto, MTProtoAnalytics, VisualShell, MatrixBackground, shared admin CSS, and protected backend/deploy/runtime guard. Post-commit Phase-58 smoke passed after HEAD included Phase-59 user frontend changes.
evidence.evidence-5: 2026-06-04 pass-local: Phase-62 compactness/deletion audit folded MTProto alert archive, MTProto storage details, and legacy VPN server compatibility diagnostics into compact secondary panels while preserving emergency controls, alert actions, confirmation/redaction guards, bounded inventories, and read-only analytics. Frontend-admin build, Phase-62 smoke, Phase-58/54/61/60 regressions, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff --check, and protected backend/deploy/runtime/frontend-user guard passed. Implementation commit: 3d09fa8.
evidence.evidence-6: 2026-06-04 pass-local-rainfix: Phase-58 protected surface guard was narrowed to permit only the shared user VisualShell Matrix rain reliability diff during Phase-60 regression. Phase-60 QA passed and protected backend/deploy/runtime guard remained clean.
evidence.evidence-7: 2026-06-05 pass-local: Phase-74 frontend-admin build and admin shell smoke passed for Logout under Nodes, removed visible Tariffs route/nav, safe /plans redirect, repaired content scroll, and bounded rail geometry.
evidence.evidence-8: 2026-06-05 pass-local: Phase-75 frontend-admin build and admin Users/Devices smoke passed for single Users inventory, removed visible Devices nav, safe /devices redirect, preserved emergency device actions, bounded expanded devices, and protected backend/deploy/runtime/user frontend guard.

V-M-076.autonomous-execution-safety: Safe for local autonomous execution if changes stay inside frontend-admin cockpit routes/components/CSS, Phase-62 smoke verification, and MyGRACE docs. Stop execution if backend admin API changes, auth/session changes, billing catalog mutation, VPN/MTProto runtime changes, deploy/install/nginx/docker-compose changes, user frontend changes, or secret-handling changes appear necessary.

phase-74-verification-design.scope: Premium admin cockpit verification for the approved navigation and scroll repair wave.
phase-74-verification-design.gate-1: Visible Tariffs UI is removed from admin only; admin shell remains compact and usable.
phase-74-verification-design.gate-2: Logout under Nodes is asserted by source order, visible label order, and route shell smoke.
phase-74-verification-design.gate-3: Scroll and rail CSS markers are required before claiming the mouse-wheel fix.
phase-74-verification-design.commands: cd frontend-admin && npm run build; node scripts/phase74-admin-shell-navigation-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase58-premium-admin-cockpit-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase62-compactness-deletion-smoke.mjs

V-M-076.phase-74-evidence: Phase-74 smoke passed PremiumAdminCockpit markers LOGOUT_UNDER_NODES, TARIFFS_ROUTE_REMOVED, and CONTENT_SCROLL_REPAIRED while protecting backend/deploy/runtime/user tariff surfaces.
V-M-076.phase-75-evidence: Phase-75 smoke passed PremiumAdminCockpit markers USERS_INVENTORY_UNIFIED, DEVICES_NAV_REMOVED, EMERGENCY_DEVICE_ACTIONS_PRESERVED, and BOUNDED_EXPANDED_DEVICES while protecting backend/deploy/runtime/user frontend surfaces.
